key_insight: A machine-learning pipeline combining imputation, imbalance handling, feature selection, and ensemble/ANN models was built to predict early Alzheimer’s disease and highlight key biomarkers.
clinical_relevance: Earlier identification of likely Alzheimer’s cases could support timelier assessment and care planning, especially when symptoms are still subtle.
method: Used ADNI clinical, neuropsychological, and neuroimaging-related data.
Applied iterative imputation and Borderline SVM-SMOTE to address missing values and class imbalance.
Trained a stacking ensemble (Logistic Regression, Extra Trees, Bagging KNN, LightGBM) and an artificial neural network after feature selection and scaling.
evidence_signal: Models were compared using precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC on train/test splits of the processed ADNI dataset.
watch_item: Because the approach depends on retrospective ADNI data and complex preprocessing, external validation is needed before real-world use.
paper_title: Predicting Early Stages Of Alzheimer's Disease And Identifying Key Biomarkers Using Deep Artificial Neural Network And Ensemble Of Machine Learning Methodologies
